headline: Quant funds suffer worst momentum rout of the year
detail: When quant strategies unwind momentum, they sell their biggest winners first - which is exactly what your book is made of: high-momentum tech and chip names. CRWD, SMH, SOXL are all momentum darlings and directly in the line of fire. This isn't a fundamental story, but forced selling doesn't care about fundamentals.
rec: Consider trimming SOXL partially near current levels ($236) before it tests $200 - you're already down 7% on a 3x leveraged name on margin, and momentum unwinds can accelerate. Scanner has no SOXL setup supporting a re-add right now.

headline: Inflation likely to stay significantly above target, top European central banker warns
detail: Sticky inflation signals higher-for-longer rates globally, which compresses valuations on growth and tech stocks - your entire concentrated book. It also raises the cost of the margin you're carrying. Not a one-day crisis, but it's a persistent headwind.
rec: No immediate trade, but this reinforces keeping position sizes in check and not adding leverage. If this theme builds, QQQ below $700 or SMH breaking $540 would be your early warning levels to act.

headline: Digital Realty falls 5% after Blackstone data center stake
detail: A 5% drop in a major data center REIT on a deal day is a warning sign for sentiment around AI infrastructure spending - the same theme driving chips and cloud names in your book like SMH and SOXL. Markets may be questioning whether the infrastructure buildout is getting overpriced.
rec: No direct trade, but watch SMH and SOXL for any sympathy weakness - if chips start pricing in slower data center capex, your stop levels on both become more important, not less.

headline: Strait of Hormuz reopening faster than expected
detail: Faster Hormuz reopening means lower oil prices, which is modestly good for tech - lower energy costs reduce input costs and ease inflation pressure slightly. Modest positive for your growth-heavy book, but not a strong enough catalyst to change today's cautious posture.
rec: This is a mild background positive for tech valuations but not a reason to add anything today given the momentum unwind risk dominating the tape right now.
